frameworks.name: neural population tuning
frameworks.one_line: sharpness of population response curves mediates anatomy-to-behavior link

frameworks.name: cortical anatomy decomposition (thickness vs surface area)
frameworks.one_line: treats thickness and surface area as genetically independent anatomical axes

frameworks.name: psychophysics of perceptual acuity
frameworks.one_line: individual perceptual discrimination across visual field positions

core_question: How do cortical thickness and surface area of early visual cortex relate to neural population tuning and perceptual acuity?
key_assumptions: cortical thickness and surface area are genetically independent variables
population tuning sharpness indexes early visual coding fidelity
perceptual discrimination accuracy reflects underlying cortical response properties
manipulations: individual differences in early visual cortical thickness
individual differences in early visual cortical surface area
visual field position for discrimination measurements
key_findings: thicker early visual cortex correlates with broader population tuning and lower discrimination accuracy
larger early visual cortical surface area correlates with sharper population tuning and higher discrimination accuracy
thickness and surface area exert opposite influences on tuning and behavior
interpretations: population tuning links visual cortical anatomy to perception
a perceptually advantageous early visual cortex is thinned with enlarged surface area
